label: U.S. real food-price index
description: Real food-price index = food CPI / all-items CPI × 100.
definition: Real food-price index = food CPI / all-items CPI × 100.
comparisonLimitations: Country baskets, weights, index bases, seasonal treatment, and housing institutions differ. Compare growth rates and within-country percentiles before levels.
interpretation: Consumer affordability depends on price growth relative to wages or income, not the price index alone.
source: Official U.S. source distributed through Federal Reserve Economic Data; Official U.S. source distributed through Federal Reserve Economic Data
revisionStatus: Derived ratio. The originating BLS, FHFA, or BEA series may be revised; FRED observations do not provide a complete publication-vintage archive here. The originating BLS, FHFA, or BEA series may be revised; FRED observations do not provide a complete publication-vintage archive here.
